title: Precipitation
definition: A reaction in which mixing two solutions produces an insoluble solid — the precipitate — that drops out of solution. Whether a solid forms, and which one, is read from the solubility rules; the amount that forms is then pure ledger. In the lesson, $\text{Ca}^{2+}$ and $\text{CO}_3^{2-}$ meet and leave solution as solid $\text{CaCO}_3$, while the spectator ions stay dissolved.
